rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR WEBB VS. WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BD. SAN FRANCISCO,CA
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 2, 2007, LINE 9. DEFENDANT WORKER'S COMPENSATION APPEALS BOARD OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. LABOR CODE SECTION 5955 PRECLUDES THE COURT FROM EXERCISING JURISDICTION OVER A CASE THAT CHALLENGE WHAT TRANSPIRED IN A W.C.A.B. PROCEEDING. THAT IS THE ESSENCE OF THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. PLAINTIFF HAS NO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION BECAUSE THE DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT PROSECUTED AN ACTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT OBTAINED A FAVORABLE OUTCOME AND DEFENDANTS ARE IMMUNE FROM PROSECUTION. REGAN V PRICE (2005) 131 CAL. APP. 4TH 1491, 1495; GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 821.6, 820.4 AND 820.2. =(302/PJM/CB-X)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST SPECIALTY INSURANCE CORPORATION VS. PEAK ENGINEERING INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Pltf'S Declaraory Relief Action Pending Resolution Of Underlying Third Party Lawsuit, Or In The Alternative, To Stay Discovery
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 2, 2007, LINE 10. DEFENDANT PEAK ENGINEERING, INC.'S Motion To Stay PLAINTIFF'S Declaratory Relief Action Pending Resolution Of Underlying Third Party Lawsuit, Or In The Alternative, To Stay Discovery: MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TAKES INCONSISTENT POSITIONS. ON ONE HAND, THIS ACTION IS BASED SIMPLY ON THE "ALLEGATIONS" IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION AND ON THE OTHER HAND, PLAINTIFF ASSERTS THE STAY WILL PRECLUDE "DISCOVERY TO VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES". THE LATTER POSITION DEMONSTRATES THE OVERLAP BETWEEN THIS CASE AND THE UNDERLYING CASE. THROUGH ITS POSITION IN THE UNDERLYING CASE, PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO "VERIFY AND DOCUMENT THE ALLEGED DAMAGES" AND WILL SUFFER NO ADVERSE CONSEQUENCE AS A RESULT OF THE STAY. THE ARBITRATION WILL CLARIFY THE ACTUAL DAMAGES AND THE CAUSES THEREOF AND WILL PROVIDE A BASIS FOR DETERMINING COVERAGE. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: MARIEL HERNANDEZ VS. MARILU ANGELES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, AUGUST 2, 2007, LINE 12. DEFENDANTS MARILU ANGELES, METZI HERNANDEZ AND EMELIN HERNANDEZ'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Defendants discharged their duty to plaintiff under Ruttenberg v. Ruttenberg (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 801 by naming and serving plaintiff in the wrongful death action as disclosed by the complaint. Since all of plaintiff's claims turn on this alleged duty, the demurrer is sustained as to the entire complaint. In determining whether plaintiff could amend, the Court has taken judicial notice of certain pleadings in the underlying case - D, E, F and N. Those records confirm plaintiff's service of process, plaintiff's filing of a cross-complaint and plaintiff's recovery of a portion of the settlement after a contested hearing. =(302/PJM/PB)
